name: Retain or Consolidate? Budget-Dependent Operator Selection for Language Agent Memory
summary: arXiv:2607.17545v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Language agents depend on memory across interactions. However, the limited context windows of large language models (LLMs) and their inference co...
description: arXiv:2607.17545v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Language agents depend on memory across interactions. However, the limited context windows of large language models (LLMs) and their inference costs constrain how much memory can be used at once. Existing systems mainly follow two strategies: memory retention and memory consolidation. Retention keeps raw records and preserves exact details, bu...
author: Qingcan Kang, Mingyang Liu, Shixiong Kai, Kaichao Liang, Zhentao Tang, Yuqi Cui, Tao Zhong, Mingxuan Yuan
